event: Karla Vita Lukan is born in Prudina. Later biographies disagree over the district, hospital and exact hour.
A school cassette is labelled 'Karlyta' by a teacher. The doubled T appears years later as a trademark.
First surviving local broadcast. She sings over a damaged backing track and continues after the host cuts her microphone.
National breakthrough. Press coverage alternates between prodigy, scandal and provincial novelty.
First performance at a state reception. Her later complaint identifies this season as the beginning of private pressure.
The alleged coercive encounter after a restricted performance at North House.
The phrase 'national daughter' becomes a recurring television caption without her approval.
FRIENDLY STAKES, a jazz-pop television special built around a poker table, is praised abroad and mocked domestically.
A six-minute live interview is rebroadcast for weeks as proof that she is unstable; the missing eleven minutes surface later.
KARLYTTA sessions recorded. The public master and longer session file acquire conflicting histories.
FORT UMBRALIS, a state-funded symphonic work, premieres once and is then removed from schedules for 'technical revision'.
MEMORIA MARIS expands into an opera-film and becomes her first work treated seriously by institutions that previously rejected her.
During the Third War, ZRAKOPERKA is used in emergency broadcasts. Karlytta later says the state turned a mourning record into recruitment music.
She walks out of NARODNA KUĆA after a host asks whether trauma makes her more commercially valuable.
ANESTEZIJA era. Several songs foreground bodies, procedures, clocks, signals and the grammar of permission.
Karlytta files suit against Ognjen Varda, the Office of the President, RP Network and the Varda Cultural Foundation.
